fields.summary: Add configuration to initialize RM with configured labels.
(Clone of YARN-796) Allow for (admin) labels on nodes and resource-requests

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

fields.description: At present, the only way to create labels is using admin API. Sometimes, there is a requirement to start the cluster with pre-configured node labels. This Jira introduces yarn configurations to start RM with predefined node labels.

This message was automatically generated.

The patch looks pretty straight-forward.
+1 from me. Would like [~leftnoteasy] to take a quick look too though - since I am wondering why this was not already supported, any specific reason why we don't currently allow specifying in the config ?

[~asuresh], [~abmodi], 

We thought about this when do initial design of node label.

Problem of doing this is, node labels could be removed after RM restart. 

For example:
{code:java}
In config, preconfigure a/b/c
After RM start, add d and remove a {code}
In this case, what we should do after RM restart? Should we read from config again or skip read the config and only read from node label store? 

We have to deal with some other corner cases so we don't do it in the beginning. 

I will be fine if you could think it through about corner cases and how users to use this feature.

Thanks [~asuresh] and [~leftnoteasy] for review. 

in case of RM restart, it will reinitialize the labels from the config and then will rerun the state from node label store. So in the case you mentioned, after RM restart, labels in the RM would be b, c and d. This should also be desired behavior.

I can create another config to delete the previously stored state and that flag can be used to restart RM with fresh config.

Thoughts?

The patch looks pretty straight-forward.
+1 from me. Would like [~leftnoteasy] to take a quick look too though - since I am wondering why this was not already supported, any specific reason why we don't currently allow specifying in the config ?

[~asuresh], [~abmodi], 

We thought about this when do initial design of node label.

Problem of doing this is, node labels could be removed after RM restart. 

For example:
{code:java}
In config, preconfigure a/b/c
After RM start, add d and remove a {code}
In this case, what we should do after RM restart? Should we read from config again or skip read the config and only read from node label store? 

We have to deal with some other corner cases so we don't do it in the beginning. 

I will be fine if you could think it through about corner cases and how users to use this feature.

Thanks [~asuresh] and [~leftnoteasy] for review. 

in case of RM restart, it will reinitialize the labels from the config and then will rerun the state from node label store. So in the case you mentioned, after RM restart, labels in the RM would be b, c and d. This should also be desired behavior.

I can create another config to delete the previously stored state and that flag can be used to restart RM with fresh config.

Thoughts?
